title: Competitor Price Effects
description: Order of operations matters because competitors can change prices in a town through their sale actions. Players should prioritize high-value exchanges near the start of their route or risk devaluation by supply and demand changes from competitor actions.
definition-of-done: Competitor actions affect city prices. Turn order determines who acts first. Price changes from sales are visible. Route optimization considers competitor risk.
testing-plan: Verify competitor sales affect prices. Test turn order impact. Verify price change visibility. Test optimization accounts for competition.
todos: Build price change from sales mechanics
Design competitor risk in route planning
